title: Understand: nuclear fission
description: Check your understanding of nuclear fission in this set of free practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

When a nucleus undergoes fission, it produces [[☃ dropdown 1]] product nuclei and a variable number of free [[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: During nuclear fission, a nucleus splits into two nuclei.
This process also produces between one and three neutral subatomic particles.
So, when a nucleus undergoes fission, it produces two product nuclei and a variable number of free neutrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

A fissile nucleus may be split into [[☃ dropdown 1]] product nuclei. This process releases between 1-3 free  [[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: During nuclear fission, a nucleus splits into two nuclei.
This process also produces between one and three neutral subatomic particles.
So, when a nucleus undergoes fission, it produces two product nuclei and a variable number of free neutrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the statement.**

When a nucleus undergoes fission, it produces two [[☃ dropdown 1]] and a variable number of free [[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: During nuclear fission, a nucleus splits into two nuclei.
This process also produces between one and three neutral subatomic particles.
So, when a nucleus undergoes fission, it produces two product nuclei and a variable number of free neutrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A researcher wishes to start a fission reaction in a controlled test reactor. 

**Complete the statement.**

* In order to start the reaction, fissile nuclei must be bombarded with free $\_\_\_\_\_\_.$

[[☃ radio 1]]

A scientist wishes to begin a fission reaction in a nuclear reactor. 

**Complete the statement.**

* In order to start the reaction, fissile nuclei must be $\_\_\_\_\_\_.$

[[☃ radio 1]]

A nuclear reactor is fueled by a fissile isotope such as $\ce{U-235}$.

**Complete the statement.**

* In order to start a chain reaction, the nuclear fuel must be bombarded with $\_\_\_\_\_\_.$

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Complete the statement.**

* Fissile isotopes are primarily used $\_\_\_\_\_\_.$

[[☃ radio 1]]

Uranium-$235$ is a fissile isotope.

**In which location would you most likely find $\ce{U-235}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a hospital that uses medical isotopes
a middle-aged star like the sun

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the primary use of fissile nuclei?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They produce energy in nuclear reactors.
They are used in radiometric dating.
They are combined to form larger nuclei.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens in a nuclear fission chain reaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A neutron produced by one fission triggers the fission of another nucleus.
Alpha decay is followed by beta decay, then by gamma decay.
Smaller nuclei are fused into progressively larger nuclei.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nuclear reactors work because each fission reaction produces free neutrons, which go on to trigger additional fission reactions. 

**What is this process called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a fission chain reaction, the fission of one nucleus triggers the fission of other nuclei. Nuclear reactors rely on chain reactions to generate power. 

 **What part of the fission process is critical to sustaining a chain reaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Each fission reaction produces between 1-3 free neutrons.
Each fission reaction produces two fission products.
